alerts.title: Iran's nuclear breakout window narrows to 1-2 weeks amid deal tensions
alerts.summary: Global metrics confirm Iran's uranium enrichment now at 60% U-235 with estimated breakout time of 1-2 weeks. This represents critical threshold amid ongoing dispute over deal implementation—White House conditionality statement and leaked MoU containing zero nuclear commitments from Iran create escalation risk (global_metrics, telegram sources).

alerts.title: Israeli strikes continue in Lebanon despite Trump pressure; Iran renews retaliation threat
alerts.summary: Israeli Air Force conducted fresh drone strikes in southern Lebanon killing at least 4 civilians (Reuters, 2026-06-17). Iran's military directly threatened 'harsh response' if attacks persist, explicitly linking escalation to potential derailment of US-Iran nuclear agreement. Trump publicly criticized Netanyahu but operations continue (incidents, telegram).

alerts.title: Iran-linked actors claim FBI drone hack; threaten World Cup infrastructure
alerts.summary: Iran-linked cyber group has claimed successful compromise of FBI drone systems and made threats against World Cup infrastructure, per France 24 and CBS News (global_metrics). Represents escalating cyber threat alongside conventional and proxy activity, indicating diversified Iranian response options amid deal implementation disputes.

alerts.title: Trump threatens to resume bombing campaign if Iran escalates beyond current violations
alerts.summary: Trump issued warning via The Guardian (incidents feed, 2026-06-17) stating he will resume bombing if Iran 'acts up,' directly referencing Iran's nightly drone attacks on Strait of Hormuz shipping since MoU signing. Statement signals fragility of ceasefire arrangement and creates tit-for-tat escalation cycle amid nuclear negotiations.

alerts.title: Three US carrier strike groups maintain forward deployment across critical zones
alerts.summary: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Red Sea), USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea), and USS Gerald R. Ford (Mediterranean) remain actively deployed in three adjacent theaters. Deployment posture maintains strike capability against Iran while supporting Israel and Gulf operations, underpinning Trump's credible bombing threat amid nuclear deal implementation crisis (assets feed).

analysis_note: US-Iran nuclear deal framework deteriorating rapidly: Iranian uranium enrichment at 1-2 week breakout threshold, Israeli strikes threatening deal viability, cyber escalation underway, and Trump threatening resumed bombing—creating multi-domain escalation risk with minimal diplomatic off-ramps.
